strategy2025.introTitle: It is a roadmap to activate the Institute's role as a leading institution in Arab urban development.
strategy2025.introSubtitle: The strategy is built on three of the Institute's strengths:

pillars.text: A window for Arab cities to contribute to shaping an urban agenda and supporting global sustainability efforts.
The first specialized institution in Arab urban development since 1980, with a membership of more than 650 Arab cities.
A regional center supporting partnerships between cities and guiding urban development efforts across the Arab world.

items.title: Vision
items.content: A leading global institution contributing to building a better urban future based on innovation and partnership between cities and development entities in the Arab world.

items.title: Goals
columns: Strengthening partnerships between member cities and organizations
Providing quality training programs and platforms
Responding to the needs and aspirations of cities

items.title: Governance and Institutional Development
items.description: We are committed to applying governance principles to ensure transparency and accountability, supporting an effective and stable work environment. Through this, we enhance institutional development efforts to improve performance efficiency and achieve sustainable growth.

items.title: Urban Resilience
tags: Climate change and natural disaster management
Water, food, energy, and waste management
items.description: This focus area aims to build more sustainable and resilient cities through smart planning and strengthening infrastructure capable of facing crises and recovering from them quickly.

items.title: Local Economic Development
items.description: We seek to enhance the economic competitiveness of Arab cities by supporting local projects, developing investment opportunities, and building effective public-private partnerships to achieve sustainable economic growth.

items.title: Cities for People
items.description: We focus on improving quality of life in Arab cities through planning that responds to residents' needs, providing safe public spaces, and strengthening community participation in city design and development.
